titleStmt.title: SII 1.27: original edition by Eugen Julius Theodor Hultzsch
No. 27. ROUND THE OUTSIDE OF THE SHRINE OF MAHENDRAVARMEŇöVARA.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
ŇõrńębhńĀradvńĀjagotrakŠĻ£itidharaŇõiravara * * * * * * *
prńĀdurbhŇęto mahendraŠł• pratinŠĻõpatigajatrńĀsivńęryyorjjitńĀdyaŠł• .
EtattenopakaŠĻáThe vihitamanupamaŠĻĀ rńĀjasiŠĻĀheŇõvarasya
prńętyńĀ nityammahendreŇõvaramadhivasatu sthńĀŠĻáurńęŇõassurńĀ[dyaŠł• . 1*]
lokńĀdityńĀtprasŇęto raŠĻáarasikacamŇępaŠĻÖkaŇõoŠĻ£ipratńĀpńĀ
dyandevo rńĀjasiŠĻĀhastanayamalabhata ŇõrńęmahendrńĀbhidhńĀnam .
tena ŇõrńęrńĀjasiŠĻĀheŇõvaragŠĻõhavihite ŇõrńęmahendreŇõvaresmi
nnńĀvńĀse kŠĻõttivńĀsńĀŠł• saha guhapariŠĻ£anmaŠĻáŠłćalaiŠł• sannidhattńĀm . 2
yaŠĻĀ rńĀjńĀ rńĀjasiŠĻĀhastanayamajanayanmedinńęvńęrasiŠĻĀho
vŠĻõttairaŠĻĀhonivŠĻõttaiŠł• kŠĻõtayugamaparannirmmimńĀŠĻáo mahendram .
tenedaŠĻĀ rńĀjasiŠĻĀheŇõvaranikaŠĻ≠asamutthńĀpitaŠĻĀ saprasńĀdo
nityńĀvńĀsammahendreŇõvaragŠĻõhamumayńĀ sńĀrddhamńęŇõo vidhattńĀm . 3
karotu kńĀlńĀntakaraŠł• purńĀntako maheŇõvarassarvvasurńĀsurńĀŇõrayaŠł• .
padaŠĻĀ sadńĀ * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * [. 4*]
mahendravarmmeŇõvaragŠĻõhamThe name of the temple, mahendravarmmeŇõvaragŠĻõham, is also engraved on either side of the staircase, which leads to the entrance of the shrine. .

(Verse 1.) May the motionless, the lord, the first of gods for ever joyfully dwell in this matchless (temple of) MahendreŇõvara, which was constructed near (the temple of) RńĀjasiŠĻĀheŇõvara by Mahendra, who sprang ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ (from) the chief of the princes of the holy BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra, from that Ň™rjita,This biruda of RńĀjasiŠĻĀha occurs also in No. 25, niche 2. whose bravery frightened the elephants of rival kings!

(2.) May the skin-robed together with the troops of his attendants, the Guhas, be present at this dwelling, (called) the holy MahendreŇõvara, which was constructed (near) the temple of the holy RńĀjasiŠĻĀheŇõvara by the illustrious Mahendra, the son of king RńĀjasiŠĻĀha, who sprang from that LokńĀditya (i.e., the sun of the world), whose valour dried up the army of RaŠĻáarasika, just as the heat of the sun does the mud!

(3.) May ń™Ňõa together with UmńĀ graciously take for his permanent dwelling this temple of MahendreŇõvara, which was erected near RńĀjasiŠĻĀheŇõvara by Mahendra, the son of king RńĀjasiŠĻĀha, the lion among the heroes of the earth, who produced another KŠĻõita age by his sinless conduct!
(4.) May MaheŇõvara, the refuge of all gods and demons, who puts an end to time and has made an end of (the demon) Pura, always (take up) his residence ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ ‚ÄĘ
Digital edition of SII 1.27 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
